title: carex flava subsp. orthorrhyncha
intro: Carex flava subsp. orthorrhyncha, also known as the yellow sedge, is a subspecies of sedge that belongs to the family Cyperaceae. It is a perennial plant with a yellowish-green color and is commonly found in wetland areas. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been shown to aid in the treatment of various health conditions, including digestive issues and respiratory problems. The yellow sedge can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, making it a popular choice among herbalists and naturopaths.
